series.title: [PATCH] target/mips: save CP0 timer in vmstate

review.summary: The change is mechanically small and matches established QEMU migration patterns for timer-backed CPU state. Adding `env.timer` as an optional subsection addresses the reported post-load hang without perturbing KVM cases where the timer is absent. I did not identify a correctness, compatibility, or maintainability issue in the patch itself.

A.details: The bug description is coherent: if the MIPS CP0 timer is not part of VMState, the restored CPU state can retain timer-related register contents while losing the scheduled host-side `QEMUTimer` expiry, which can stall guest progress until another event wakes the main loop.

B.details: The patch adds a dedicated timer subsection using `VMSTATE_TIMER_PTR(env.timer, MIPSCPU)`, which is the normal QEMU mechanism for serializing a pre-existing `QEMUTimer *`. The `.needed` callback correctly suppresses the subsection when `env.timer` is absent.

C.details: Using an optional subsection is the right compatibility shape for extending VMState. New QEMU can still load old snapshots without the timer subsection, and the null-timer/KVM case is kept out of the stream.

D.details: The added include and subsection are localized to MIPS system migration code. No suspicious interactions with unrelated CPU state, reset paths, or TLB/FPU serialization are introduced by this diff.

E.details: `checkpatch`, git-am, and configure all succeed, and the code pattern mirrors existing timer VMState handling used by other targets. Residual risk is low and mainly tied to runtime coverage rather than code shape.

diff:
From git@z Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
Subject: [PATCH] target/mips: save CP0 timer in vmstate
From: Trieu Huynh <vikingtc4@gmail.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Mar 2026 20:37:32 +0900
Message-Id: <20260329113732.482619-1-vikingtc4@gmail.com>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

The MIPS R4K CP0 timer (env->timer) is not included in vmstate_mips_cpu,
so after loadvm the QEMUTimer has no scheduled expiry. This causes
qemu_poll_ns() to block indefinitely and the guest to freeze until an
external I/O event (e.g. a keypress) wakes the main loop.

Fix by adding an optional vmstate subsection for the timer, following
the same pattern used by ARM (gt_timer), RISC-V (env.stimer), SPARC
(qtimer), and OpenRISC (timer).

The .needed callback returns false when env->timer is NULL (KVM mode),
keeping the subsection optional for backwards compatibility with
existing snapshots.

nit: when trying to reproduce of this bug requires savevm to work on
MIPS Malta. That is currently broken by a separate crash in
vmsd_can_compress() introduced by commit 7aa563630b ("pc: Start with
modern CPU hotplug interface by default"), which is being tracked and
fixed independently [1], [2]. This fix is independent of that crash and
touches no shared code.

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/qemu-devel/20260316194759.22672-1-farosas@suse.de/
[2] https://lore.kernel.org/qemu-devel/87341zsvmc.fsf@suse.de/

Resolves: https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/issues/1987

Signed-off-by: Trieu Huynh <vikingtc4@gmail.com>
---
 target/mips/system/machine.c | 22 ++++++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 22 insertions(+)

diff --git a/target/mips/system/machine.c b/target/mips/system/machine.c
index 8af11fd896..473d3ab036 100644
--- a/target/mips/system/machine.c
+++ b/target/mips/system/machine.c
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@
 #include "internal.h"
 #include "migration/cpu.h"
 #include "fpu_helper.h"
+#include "qemu/timer.h"
 
 static int cpu_post_load(void *opaque, int version_id)
 {
@@ -219,6 +220,23 @@ static const VMStateDescription vmstate_tlb = {
 
 /* MIPS CPU state */
 
+static bool mips_timer_needed(void *opaque)
+{
+    MIPSCPU *cpu = opaque;
+    return cpu->env.timer != NULL;
+}
+
+static const VMStateDescription mips_vmstate_timer = {
+    .name = "cpu/timer",
+    .version_id = 1,
+    .minimum_version_id = 1,
+    .needed = mips_timer_needed,
+    .fields = (const VMStateField[]) {
+        VMSTATE_TIMER_PTR(env.timer, MIPSCPU),
+        VMSTATE_END_OF_LIST()
+    }
+};
+
 const VMStateDescription vmstate_mips_cpu = {
     .name = "cpu",
     .version_id = 21,
@@ -333,4 +351,8 @@ const VMStateDescription vmstate_mips_cpu = {
 
         VMSTATE_END_OF_LIST()
     },
+    .subsections = (const VMStateDescription * const []) {
+        &mips_vmstate_timer,
+        NULL
+    }
 };
-- 
2.43.0
disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
